title: Podman doesn't create .bp.sock in long path when rootless
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When running podman in rootless mode with long XDG_RUNTIME_DIR with option `-p 8080:8080`, socket file `.bp.sock` isn't created, causing podman to fail. In my testing I've found that max path length for XDG_RUNTIME_DIR seems to be 66 characters, after `libpod/tmp/rootlessport[0-9]{9}` directory is created the resulting path has 99 characters, with this podman still runs fine, but once the resulting path reaches 100 characters (67 char XDG_RUNTIME_DIR) the socket isn't created.

However .bp-ready.pipe is created.

I have also tried creating a sock file using python sockets in the long path and that worked fine, so I don't think there are any problems with filesystem/OS.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a directory with absolute path that has at least 67 characters, e.g. `/tmp/thisisasuperlongdirectorythatcausespodmantonotcreateasockandfail`

2. set XDG_RUNTIME_DIR to this newly created directory

3. run podman in rootless mode and publish a port

**Describe the results you received:**
Error: rootlessport dial unix $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/libpod/tmp/rootlessport747710965/.bp.sock: connect: invalid argument

**Describe the results you expected:**
.bp.sock should be created in the directory

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.0.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

comments.body:
> I don't think there are any problems with filesystem/OS.

The max length of socket path is 108 on Linux, so the error is expected.


https://man7.org/linux/man-pages/man7/unix.7.html

Oh, I see, when I was testing in python I did bind("./.bp.sock") in the directory thinking that it might result in a similar error, but it worked. When I tried bind with absolute path it also failed on `path too long`. Thanks for pointing that out!
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Closing as this is not a podman issue.
